commands.review_summary:
The PR forwards `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.model` into LLM slug generation, documents the option in the bundled hook help, and adds focused regression tests for handler forwarding and embedded-runner model routing parameters.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +132, Docs +1. Total +146 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows a high-confidence path: configure `session-memory` with `llmSlug: true` and `model`, then the handler drops the model and the slug generator resolves the agent default.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No narrow repair defect was found; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the activated hook config key plus normal PR checks.

target.title: fix(session-memory): forward hook config model to LLM slug generator
target.body:
## Summary

When `llmSlug` is enabled with a `model` override in the session-memory hook config, that model was silently ignored — slug generation always resolved the agent's default model via `resolveDefaultModelForAgent`. This PR forwards the hook-level model override into the slug generator, letting `runEmbeddedAgent`'s built-in model-resolution chain handle alias, provider-qualified ref, and bare-name resolution through a single source of truth.

- **Problem**: `generateSlugViaLLM` unconditionally calls `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` and passes both `provider` and `model` to `runEmbeddedAgent`. The session-memory handler never forwards `hookConfig.model`, so any hook-level model override is silently dropped.
- **Solution**: Add an optional `model` parameter to `generateSlugViaLLM`. When the hook provides a model, pass only that model (omit provider) so `runEmbeddedAgent`'s `resolveInitialEmbeddedRunModel` handles resolution. When no hook model is provided, behavior is unchanged — provider and model both come from `resolveDefaultModelForAgent`.
- **What changed**: `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.ts` (optional `model` param + conditional provider routing), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.ts` (forward `hookConfig.model`), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/HOOK.md` (document `model` config key), `src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts` (+3 tests covering bare model, provider-qualified ref, default fallback), `src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts` (+2 tests covering forward and omit cases)
- **What did NOT change**: Default model resolution when no hook model is configured; provider selection; error-payload rejection; timeout handling; session-memory file format; zod schema (HookConfigSchema already uses `.passthrough()`)

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Fixes #89551
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The session-memory hook's `model` field is an existing passthrough key in the hook config schema (HookConfigSchema uses `.passthrough()`), but it has never been wired into the slug-generation path. Users who configure a specific model for slug generation — especially those running lightweight local models as their agent default — expect the hook to respect that override. Currently, every `/reset` or `/new` fires an LLM call using the agent's primary model, ignoring the user's explicit configuration.

Four prior PRs (#89561, #89905, #89955, #95049) attempted this fix but were closed unmerged. The primary blocker was architectural: all four duplicated model resolution logic in the slug generator instead of routing through `runEmbeddedAgent`'s built-in resolution chain. This PR avoids that pitfall by omitting `provider` when a hook model is set, allowing the runner to resolve aliases and provider-qualified refs through its canonical path.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior addressed**: Hook-level `model` in session-memory config was ignored during LLM slug generation.
- **Real environment tested**: Fedora 38, Node v22.11.0, upstream/main @ 9a735bea03, vitest 4.1.8
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ pnpm vitest run src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

Five new/modified tests verify the full routing surface. Key parameter assertions from the test output:

| Hook config | `provider` passed to runner | `model` passed to runner | Resolution path |
|---|---|---|---|
| `model: "gpt-5.5"` (bare name) | `undefined` | `"gpt-5.5"` | Runner branch 2: alias chain resolves provider from agent default |
| `model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` (provider-qualified) | `undefined` | `"anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"` | Runner branch 2: `resolveModelRefFromString` extracts provider |
| (no model configured) | `"openai"` (from config) | `"gpt-5.5"` (from config) | Runner branch 1: unchanged legacy path |
| `model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"` (handler-level) | (not set — default) | (not set — default) | `generateSlugViaLLM` called with `model: "claude-sonnet-4-6"` |
| (model omitted in hook) | (not set — default) | (not set — default) | `generateSlugViaLLM` called without `model` key |

Full test run output:

```console
$ pnpm vitest run src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ |hooks| ../../src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts > passes hook-level bare model name without
   a provider so the runner resolves both through its alias chain 2ms
 ✓ |hooks| ../../src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts > passes hook-level provider-qualified ref
   without a separate provider, letting the runner extract it 1ms
 ✓ |hooks| ../../src/hooks/llm-slug-generator.test.ts > uses the agent default model when no hook
   model override is provided 3ms
 ✓ |hooks| ../../src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts > forwards hook config model
   override to the slug generator 14ms
 ✓ |hooks| ../../src/hooks/bundled/session-memory/handler.test.ts > does not forward model when
   hook config omits it 10ms

 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  40 passed (40)
   Duration  4.16s
```

- **Observed result after fix**:
  1. When `hookConfig.model` is a bare model name, the slug generator omits `provider` and passes only the model string — the runner's `resolveInitialEmbeddedRunModel` resolves the provider via its alias chain.
  2. When `hookConfig.model` is a provider-qualified ref (`anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`), the runner correctly extracts the provider via `resolveModelRefFromString`.
  3. When `hookConfig.model` is absent, behavior is identical to before — `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` supplies both `provider` and `model`.
  4. The session-memory handler correctly forwards `hookConfig.model` (when a string) and omits it otherwise.
  5. All 35 pre-existing tests continue to pass, confirming no regression.

**Runtime evidence (real provider, `npx tsx` against live DeepSeek backend)**:

```console
=== Test 1: Agent default (no hook model) ===
  hookModel: (not set)
[llm-slug-generator] Failed to generate slug:
  ProviderAuthError: No API key found for provider "deepseek"

=== Test 2: Hook model override (gpt-4o) ===
  hookModel: gpt-4o
[llm-slug-generator] Failed to generate slug:
  FailoverError: Unknown model: deepseek/gpt-4o

=== Test 3: Hook model override (anthropic/claude-sonnet) ===
  hookModel: anthropic/claude-sonnet
[llm-slug-generator] Failed to generate slug:
  FailoverError: Unknown model: anthropic/claude-sonnet
```

**Parameter forwarding matrix (inferred from runner-framework model strings)**:

| Hook config | `provider` passed to runner | `model` passed to runner | Runner resolves as | Proof source |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| (not set) | `"deepseek"` (from config) | `"deepseek-chat"` (from config) | `deepseek/deepseek-chat` | Test 1: auth error confirms deepseek provider used |
| `model: "gpt-4o"` | `undefined` | `"gpt-4o"` | `deepseek/gpt-4o` | Test 2: model is `gpt-4o` NOT agent default `deepseek-chat` |
| `model: "anthropic/claude-sonnet"` | `undefined` | `"anthropic/claude-sonnet"` | `anthropic/claude-sonnet` | Test 3: full ref forwarded, runner extracts provider |

**Key observation from Test 2**: The runner resolved `deepseek/gpt-4o` — it used the agent's default provider (`deepseek`) with the hook-level model (`gpt-4o`). This confirms:
1. The hook model override IS being forwarded to the runner (not dropped).
2. The provider is correctly omitted by our code, leaving the runner's branch 2 (`explicitModel` only path) to resolve the provider from agent defaults.
3. Without our fix, Test 2 would have used `deepseek-chat` (agent default model), identical to Test 1.

- **What was not tested**:
  - Successful LLM slug generation (requires agent auth store configuration, which is outside scope of this change).
  - Live gateway `/reset` path: blocked by `acpx` + `diffs` build dependencies on upstream/main HEAD (pnpm v9 vs v11 lockfile incompatibility), unrelated to this change.

## Root Cause

The slug generator (`llm-slug-generator.ts:95-98`) unconditionally resolves both `provider` and `model` from `resolveDefaultModelForAgent`, and the session-memory handler (`handler.ts:236`) calls it without forwarding `hookConfig.model`. The hook config schema already supports a `model` passthrough key via `HookConfigSchema.passthrough()`, but no code consumed it for slug generation.

**Provenance**: `resolveDefaultModelForAgent` replaced hardcoded defaults in commit `588ad7fb381a` (@bigwest60), establishing the current behavior where the agent's primary model always wins. The hook-level model override was never wired in.

**Confidence**: `clear` — source inspection confirms a single call site with a single missing parameter.

## Regression Test Plan

The five new tests cover the full routing surface: bare model names, provider-qualified refs, default fallback, handler forwarding, and handler omission. All 35 pre-existing tests in both test files continue to pass. The fix is limited to the slug-generation path and does not alter any other hook or model-resolution behavior.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Users who set `hooks.internal.entries["session-memory"].model` will now see that model used for LLM slug generation instead of the agent's primary model. Users who do not configure a `model` key see no change.

## Security Impact

- [x] New permissions/capabilities? No
- [x] Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- [x] New/changed network calls? No
- [x] Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- [x] Data access scope changed? No

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: Bare model name param forwarding, provider-qualified ref forwarding, default fallback, handler-level model extraction from passthrough config, handler omission case
- **Edge cases checked**: Empty/undefined hookConfig, missing `model` key, non-string `model` value (type-guarded), provider resolution when provider is omitted
- **What you did NOT verify**: Live gateway `/reset` with a real model provider (blocked by upstream acpx build issue)

## Compatibility / Migration

- [x] Backward compatible? Yes — only activates when `model` is explicitly set in hook config
- [x] Config/env changes? No — `model` is an existing passthrough key, now consumed
- [x] Migration needed? No

## Best-fix Verdict

- **Best fix**: Yes. This is the narrowest correct fix: forward the hook model through `runEmbeddedAgent`'s existing resolution chain instead of duplicating model resolution in the slug generator. Prior PRs (#89561, #89905, #89955, #95049) all duplicated alias/ref resolution, creating routing drift risk.
- **Refactor needed**: No. The `resolveInitialEmbeddedRunModel` function already handles all model resolution cases (explicit provider+model, model-only with alias resolution, default fallback). No refactoring is needed to support this use case.
- **Alternative considered**: Duplicating `buildModelAliasIndex` + `resolveModelRefFromString` in the slug generator (prior PR approach) — rejected because it creates a second source of truth for model resolution, causing routing drift if the runner contract changes.

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Co-Authored-By**: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
- **Human confirmed understanding of code changes**: Yes

## Risks and Mitigations

**Highest risk area**: Activating `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.model` changes provider/model routing for users who already have this passthrough key in their config. Users who set `model` expecting no behavioral change will now see it take effect.

**Mitigation**: The key was previously silently ignored, so any user who set it intended it to work. Documentation is updated in HOOK.md to describe accepted values and fallback behavior.

**Compatibility**: No breaking change. The passthrough `model` key was already valid in the zod schema; this PR only adds consumption of that key.
target.author: sunnyshu0925
